root_text.an8.53:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an8.53:1.2: Atha kho mahāpajāpatī gotamī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā mahāpajāpatī gotamī bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.53:2.1: “Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā saṅkhittena dhammaṁ desetu, yamahaṁ bhagavato dhammaṁ sutvā ekā vūpakaṭṭhā appamattā ātāpinī pahitattā vihareyyan”ti.
root_text.an8.53:3.1: Ye ca kho tvaṁ, gotami, dhamme jāneyyāsi:

translation_text.an8.53:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an8.53:1.2: Then Mahāpajāpati Gotamī went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an8.53:2.1: “Sir, may the Buddha please teach me Dhamma in brief. When I’ve heard it, I’ll live alone, withdrawn, diligent, keen, and resolute.”
translation_text.an8.53:2.2: “Gotamī, you might know that certain things
translation_text.an8.53:2.7: to lack of contentment, not contentment;
translation_text.an8.53:2.10: to being burdensome, not being unburdensome.
translation_text.an8.53:2.11: Categorically, you should remember these things as
translation_text.an8.53:2.12: not the teaching, not the training, and not the Teacher’s instructions.
translation_text.an8.53:3.1: You might know that certain things
translation_text.an8.53:3.6: to contentment, not lack of contentment;
translation_text.an8.53:3.9: to being unburdensome, not being burdensome.
translation_text.an8.53:3.10: Categorically, you should remember these things as
translation_text.an8.53:3.11: the teaching, the training, and the Teacher’s instructions.”
